name: Iran-Iraq War
description: The Iran-Iraq War (1980–1988) was an eight-year conflict initiated by Iraq's invasion of Iran in September 1980. It was the longest conventional war of the twentieth century, killing an estimated 500,000 to 1 million people on both sides. The war shaped a generation of Iranian military and political leaders, particularly those who went on to lead the [Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps). [Mir-Hossein Mousavi](/people/mir-hossein-mousavi) served as prime minister throughout the conflict. The war ended with UN-brokered ceasefire Resolution 598, which [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini) accepted, comparing it to 'drinking poison'.
